item.title: Apple Raises Prices on Most Products by 15–25 Percent, but Not iPhones, Watches, or AirPods 摘要
item.description:
Apple raised prices on Mac and iPad products by 15-25% on June 25, 2026, citing unprecedented component cost increases. The base MacBook Air rose $200 to $1,299, MacBook Pro increased $300 to $1,999, and MacBook Neo went up $100 to $699. iPad Air and Pro also saw increases. iPhone, Watch, and AirPods prices remained unchanged, but Apple hinted at future increases. The Vision Pro also saw a 6% price hike to $3,700.
标签: Apple, price increase, Mac, iPad, Vision Pro, component costs, inflation, tech industry, consumer electronics, pricing

item.title: datasette-export-database 0.3a2 摘要
item.description:
Datasette Export Database plugin version 0.3a2 fixes a dependency issue where pyproject.toml incorrectly pinned datasette to version 1.0a27, making the plugin incompatible with other Datasette versions. The fix changes the dependency to datasette>=1.0a27, allowing compatibility with all Datasette versions 1.0a27 and later.
标签: datasette, plugin, release, bugfix, dependency, python, open-source

item.title: Hart’s theorem 摘要
item.description:
Hart's theorem states that for a triangle formed by arcs of three circles, the inscribed circle and the three escribed circles are all tangent to a new circle or line. The inscribed circle is the largest circle fitting inside the three-sided figure. Escribed circles are analogous to excircles in standard geometry, tangent to one side and the extensions of the other two sides, but here the sides are circular arcs. The theorem generalizes classical triangle circle geometry to curvilinear triangles.
标签: Hart's theorem, geometry, circles, triangle, inscribed circle, escribed circle, curvilinear triangle, tangent circles, mathematics, John D. Cook

item.title: Om Malik, 1966-2026 摘要
item.description:
Om Malik, a prominent figure in the tech community, passed away on June 24, 2026, at Stanford Hospital after a long battle with heart disease. He was surrounded by family and friends. His family shared the news, noting that Om kept his health struggles private. The announcement invites remembrances via social media platforms. The article, posted on Daring Fireball, pays tribute to his life and impact.
标签: obituary, tech, Om Malik, heart disease, tribute, Daring Fireball, community, loss, journalism, blogging

item.title: AI and Liability 摘要
item.description:
Bruce Schneier discusses a landmark German ruling holding Google liable for errors in its AI overviews. He argues that AI agents should be legally treated as agents of the deploying organization, just as human employees would be. Allowing companies to avoid liability by blaming faulty AI would create perverse incentives, encouraging businesses to replace human workers with cheaper, unaccountable AI systems. The ruling sets an important precedent for AI accountability.
标签: AI, liability, law, Google, Bruce Schneier, AI ethics, generative AI, hallucinations, regulation, accountability

item.title: ★ Spensive Thoughts 摘要
item.description:
Apple raised prices on many hardware products due to increased RAM and SSD component costs. The Apple TV 4K saw the steepest percentage increases (54-67%), while Vision Pro got a modest 6% bump. iPad base model rose 29% to $450. Mac Mini and Mac Studio increased 25-33%. MacBook and iMac rose 15-20%. Notably, iPhone, Apple Watch, and AirPods prices remained unchanged, possibly until new models launch. High-end configurations like the M5 Max MacBook Pro with 128GB RAM and 8TB SSD saw up to 40% increases, with some RAM/SSD upgrade costs doubling.
标签: Apple, price increase, hardware, Mac, iPad, Apple TV, Vision Pro, RAM, SSD, component costs

item.title: Apple Journal’s Atrocious Undo Bug Has Been Fixed (and SwiftUI, Per Se, Is Not to Blame) 摘要
item.description:
Apple's Journal app had a severe undo bug where deleting a word and undoing caused the entire sentence to disappear, with the deleted word permanently lost from the undo stack. The bug was initially attributed to SwiftUI, but Apple PR clarified the text editing component uses UIKit, not SwiftUI. Apple confirmed the bug was identified and fixed for a future update, and it is now resolved in developer beta 2 of macOS and iOS 27. However, it remains broken in current 26.6 betas and all 26.5 releases, so users should be cautious.
标签: Apple, Journal, bug, undo, UIKit, SwiftUI, data loss, macOS, iOS, software quality
